title: Overriding methods should do more than simply call the same method in the super class
description: When a method is overridden just to call the same method from the super class without performing any other actions, it can be misleading and redundant. This rule flags such cases except when the method is final or when it overrides equals, hashCode and toString.
recommendation: Ensure that the overriding method performs additional actions beyond simply calling the same method in the super class, or consider removing the overriding method if it's unnecessary.
impact: Code quality can be affected as unnecessary method overriding can lead to confusion and bloated code. It can also mislead developers if they expect the overridden method to perform differently.

checks.message: Overriding methods should do more than simply call the same method in the super class
